etymology.description: semantic 糸(糸) (mì, 'silk; thread') + phonetic 己(己) (jǐ) -> record
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 糸(糸) (mì, 'silk; thread') indicating threads or lines, with 己(己) (jǐ) as the phonetic component. In this context, 'thread' aligns with 'record' in the sense of a sequence or line of events documented over time.
etymology.simplification: The traditional form 紀 with 糸(糸) (mì, 'silk; thread') replaced by the simpler 纟(纟) in the simplified form 纪.

examples.chinese: 这本书详细记录了历史事件(這本書詳細記錄了歷史事件)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū xiángxì jìlù le lìshǐ shìjiàn.
examples.english: This book records historical events in detail.

examples.chinese: 古代人通过文字来纪念重要事情(古代人通過文字來紀念重要事情)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài rén tōngguò wénzì lái jìniàn zhòngyào shìqíng.
examples.english: Ancient people recorded important events through writing.

examples.chinese: 公司在年底会发布年度报告来记录发展情况(公司在年底會發佈年度報告來記錄發展情況)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngsī zài niándǐ huì fābù niándù bàogào lái jìlù fāzhǎn qíngkuàng.
examples.english: The company will release an annual report at the end of the year to record development status.
